brief_notes: Study identifying triple-positive EpCAM+/CD24+/Vimentin+ cells as disseminating EMT CSCs at the invasive front of 84 human oral cancer specimens, with neural network prediction of metastasis (87-89% accuracy). Strengths: direct human tumour observation (not model systems), large imaging dataset (12,000 fields), clinically relevant question, clear marker combination rationale. Weaknesses: no figures showing the actual immunofluorescence staining, ANN details are extremely sparse (architecture, hyperparameters, training details missing), cross-validation scheme not specified (k-fold?), no ROC curves/AUC/sensitivity/specificity reported, the enrichment in metastatic specimens reports p<0.05 without effect size or specific statistical test, no independent validation cohort (acknowledged), marker selection rationale could be stronger. The p-value threshold of 0.05 is lenient for biomarker discovery. Statistical reporting needs improvement.
